name: Goose (Block)
pricing_notes: Fully open source; bring your own LLM API key (any OpenAI-compatible provider)

auth.notes: Framework only; configure LLM provider API key in environment or config

performance.notes: Task-dependent; Rust core provides minimal framework overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Open-source autonomous AI agent from Block (Jack Dorsey's company). Built in Rust with Python extension support. First-class MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration — designed from the ground up around MCP tools. Desktop app + CLI. Code execution, file management, shell commands built-in. Supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM.

gotchas: Rust-based — Python developers may find extension development harder than pure-Python frameworks
Younger project with smaller community than AutoGen or CrewAI
Desktop app primary UX; headless/server mode less polished
MCP-first design means best with MCP-supporting LLM providers
best_when: Teams wanting MCP-native agent framework from the ground up
Developers preferring open-source alternative to Claude Desktop/Cursor
Projects needing autonomous agent with code execution + shell access
Teams experimenting with MCP ecosystem integration
avoid_when: Production enterprise deployments requiring commercial support
Python-only stacks (Rust core is a barrier for heavy customization)
summary: Goose is Block's open-source autonomous AI agent built in Rust with first-class MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration. Provides code execution, file management, and shell access out of the box. Desktop app + CLI. Works with any OpenAI-compatible LLM. Distinguished by its MCP-native design — the first major agent framework built around MCP from the start. Best for teams building in the MCP ecosystem.
